items.title: The Fed Takeover: It's Mission Impossible

Senior U.S. Admin Official Says "Expect Trump Executive Action In Next Few Days, Will Explain Trans-Shipment Rules At That Point; Indonesia Will Drop Its Effort To Tariff The Internet, And Immediately Back Renewal Of E-commerce Moratorium At The WTO; Indonesia Will Remove Pre-shipment Inspection Requirements Of U.S. Farm Products That Had Been A Big Obstacle; Goods That Are Trans-Shipped Will Still Face A Tariff Of 40%; Indonesia Will Also Agree To Exempt U.S. Companies From Local Content Requirements"

Reuters Cites Senior U.S. Administration Official Saying "Look Forward To Implementing Deal In Coming Weeks; Indonesia Agreed To Accept U.S. FDA Certificates And Authorizations For Medical Devices And Pharmaceuticals; President Has Authority To Modify Trade Agreements If Countries Do Not Meet Their Commitments To Lower Non-Tariff Barriers; Many Countries Have Taken Steps In Advance To Lower Non-Tariff Barriers; We Will Be Monitoring For Non-compliance And Will Take Action If We Need To"
Bear Market Playbook: Decoding Recession Risk, Valuation Impact, And Style Leadership
items.description: Bear markets are notoriously nerve racking with each drawdown presenting its own set of unique circumstances. Click to read.

items.title: Reuters Cites Senior U.S. Administration Official Saying Indonesia Will Drop Its Tariffs To Zero On Over 99% Of Trade With U.S.; U.S. Will Drop U.S. Tariffs On Goods From Indonesia To 19% From 32% Initially Planned; Indonesia Will Eliminate All Non-Tariff Barriers On U.S.; Indonesia Will Accept U.S. Federal Motor Safety Standards For Vehicles Exported From U.S.; Indonesia Will Remove Export Restrictions On Critical Minerals; Deal Is Worth At Least $50B To U.S. In Terms Of Access And Purchases
